name: Pint (Physical Units Library for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); hgrecco / Hernán E. Grecco

performance.notes: Unit tracking adds overhead vs bare numerics; negligible for most uses

agent_readiness.notes: Physical units library for Python. from pint import UnitRegistry. ureg = UnitRegistry(). distance = 10 * ureg.meter. time = 5 * ureg.second. speed = distance / time → 2.0 meter/second. speed.to(ureg.km / ureg.hour) → 7.2 km/h. speed.to_base_units(). speed.magnitude → 2.0; speed.units → meter/second. ureg('60 mph').to('km/h'). ureg.Quantity(10, 'meter'). DimensionError if incompatible units added. pint.Unit. numpy integration: numpy arrays with units. pint.UnitRegistry.setup_matplotlib() for matplotlib axis labels. Pandas: pint-pandas extension. Custom units: ureg.define('fiber = 1.2 meter'). pint.util.column_echelon_form. Applications: physics, engineering, chemistry, data science.

gotchas: Unit registries are not interchangeable — don't mix Quantities from different UnitRegistry instances
Overhead vs bare numpy for large arrays
pint-pandas required for DataFrame-level unit tracking
Custom unit definitions can conflict with built-in units
best_when: Scientific Python code where unit mismatch bugs are a risk
Engineering calculations mixing metric and imperial
Data pipelines needing automatic unit conversion
Physics simulations, sensor data, IoT measurement processing
avoid_when: Performance-critical numerical code where overhead matters
Simple scripts where dimensional analysis is obvious
When unyt or astropy.units already provides the needed integration
summary: Pint tracks physical units in Python. ureg = UnitRegistry(); distance = 10 * ureg.meter; speed = distance / time → m/s; speed.to('km/h'). DimensionError for incompatible operations. numpy arrays with units. pint-pandas for DataFrames. BSD-3-Clause by hgrecco. Don't mix Quantities from different UnitRegistry instances. Best for scientific/engineering code preventing unit mismatch bugs.
